message.content:
{
  "lecture_id": "graph_algorithms_001",
  "knowledge_objects": [
    {
      "id": "weighted_directed_graph",
      "name": "Weighted Directed Graph",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A directed graph where each edge has an associated numerical weight.",
      "source_span": "A weighted directed graph assigns a numerical weight \\(w(u,v)\\) to each directed edge \\((u,v)\\)."
    },
    {
      "id": "path",
      "name": "Path",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A sequence of compatible directed edges.",
      "source_span": "A path is a sequence of compatible directed edges."
    },
    {
      "id": "path_cost",
      "name": "Path Cost",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The sum of edge weights along a path.",
      "source_span": "Its path cost is the sum of their edge weights"
    },
    {
      "id": "path_cost_formula",
      "name": "Path Cost Formula",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Formula for the cost of a path as the sum of edge weights.",
      "source_span": "C(v_0,\\ldots,v_k) = \\sum_{i=0}^{k-1} w(v_i,v_{i+1})"
    },
    {
      "id": "single_source_shortest_path_problem",
      "name": "Single-Source Shortest-Path Problem",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Problem of finding a minimum-cost path from a source vertex to every reachable vertex.",
      "source_span": "The single-source shortest-path problem asks for a minimum-cost path from a chosen source vertex to every reachable vertex."
    },
    {
      "id": "dijkstras_algorithm",
      "name": "Dijkstra's Algorithm",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Algorithm for single-source shortest paths with nonnegative edge weights using a priority queue and edge relaxation.",
      "source_span": "Dijkstra's algorithm is applied to this problem when all edge weights are nonnegative."
    },
    {
      "id": "edge_relaxation",
      "name": "Edge Relaxation",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Update operation that reduces the tentative distance to a vertex if a shorter path is found.",
      "source_span": "it applies edge relaxation to each outgoing edge."
    },
    {
      "id": "relaxation_update",
      "name": "Relaxation Update",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Update rule for tentative distance d(v) as min of current d(v) and d(u)+w(u,v).",
      "source_span": "d(v) \\leftarrow \\min\\{d(v),\\, d(u)+w(u,v)\\}"
    },
    {
      "id": "a_star_search",
      "name": "A* Search",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Extension of Dijkstra's algorithm that uses a heuristic function to guide exploration.",
      "source_span": "A* search extends Dijkstra's algorithm by using a heuristic function \\(h(v)\\) to guide which vertex is explored next."
    },
    {
      "id": "heuristic_function",
      "name": "Heuristic Function",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Function h(v) that estimates the cost from vertex v to the goal.",
      "source_span": "heuristic function \\(h(v)\\)"
    }
  ]
}
